data.name: ClassAccessibilityMessage
data.value: The class '{0}' should be public or internal to be used with [JobFunction]

data.name: MethodAccessibilityMessage
data.value: The method '{0}' should be public or internal to be used with [JobFunction]

data.name: InvalidCronExpressionMessage
data.value: The cron expression '{0}' in function '{1}' is invalid

data.name: MissingFunctionNameMessage
data.value: The [JobFunction] attribute on method '{0}' in class '{1}' must specify a function name

data.name: DuplicateFunctionNameMessage
data.value: The function name '{0}' is already used by another [JobFunction] method

data.name: MultipleConstructorsMessage
data.value: The class '{0}' has multiple constructors. Only the first constructor will be used for dependency injection. Consider using [JobsConstructor] attribute to explicitly mark the preferred constructor.

data.name: NestedClassMessage
data.value: The nested class '{0}' contains [JobFunction] methods. JobFunction methods are only allowed in top-level classes.

data.name: InvalidMethodParameterMessage
data.value: The method '{0}' has invalid parameter '{1}' of type '{2}'. JobFunction methods can only have JobFunctionContext, JobFunctionContext<T>, CancellationToken parameters, or no parameters.

data.name: MultipleJobsConstructorAttributesMessage
data.value: The class '{0}' has multiple constructors with [JobsConstructor] attribute. Only one constructor can be marked with [JobsConstructor].
